references.name: Bottega Veneta
references.takeaway: Reduce navigation to an absolute minimum (wordmark + one icon) and let each content section contain a single centered image with 70%+ negative space, making every visual feel like a museum-grade object displayed on a marble pedestal.
applied: Navigation reduced to a minimal wordmark ('Meridian') plus compact icon pills — all secondary labels hidden by default on desktop, revealed only on mobile expansion
Dashboard hero card uses massive negative space (center-aligned single stat block occupying ~30% of the card area) creating a museum-pedestal presentation for the $847M portfolio value
Gallery exhibition view displays each idea in isolated brass frames with generous padding and centered imagery, treating each investment thesis as a curated gallery object
Spacious density setting maintained throughout — generous padding on cards (28-40px), wide gaps between sections (40-48px), and centered alignment creating breathing room

references.name: Celine
references.takeaway: Set all typography in a refined grotesk typeface at two weights only (regular and bold), and use an austere black/white/cream palette where product photography provides the sole source of color warmth.
applied: While using Cinzel serif (per style direction for engraved banking feel) instead of grotesk, the two-weight discipline is maintained: Cinzel at 400 (regular) and 600-700 (bold) only, with no intermediate weights creating a refined typographic hierarchy
Austere palette of cream (#f2ece6), white (#faf7f2), and dark green (#1a4a2a) with brass gold (#b8963a) as the sole warmth accent — mirroring Celine's monochrome-plus-one-warm-tone approach
Content imagery (gallery frame illustrations, chart SVGs) provides the primary visual interest against an otherwise restrained neutral canvas

references.name: Savills Private Finance
references.takeaway: Use a deep navy and gold dual-accent system on a pure white background, and present all content in strict 12-column grid alignment with specification-style metadata (small serif type, structured key-value pairs) that conveys private-institution authority.
applied: Deep green (#1a4a2a) and gold (#b8963a) dual-accent system applied on cream/white backgrounds — adapted from navy/gold to match the marble banking direction while maintaining the same authoritative contrast structure
Ledger table uses strict columnar alignment with specification-style metadata: reference numbers (MPW-001), fund types, AUM figures, and YTD returns in structured key-value layout
Schema planner certificates present data in formal field-label/field-value pairs using small serif type (Cinzel at 11px for labels, Libre Baskerville at 14px for values)
Card catalog uses structured grid layout (reference code, title, type) echoing Savills' property card specification grids
Settings panel uses key-value rows with institutional-weight formatting — engraved section labels with brass gold-leaf dividers between groups

combinedInfluence: The design synthesizes Bottega Veneta's radical negative space and minimal navigation with Celine's disciplined two-weight typography and monochrome-plus-warmth palette, grounded in Savills' institutional data-presentation patterns. The result is a private banking interface that feels like walking through a marble-clad lobby where every element has been placed with deliberate authority and quiet luxury.
